overview: SpecForge AI is a guided workflow portal that transforms unstructured requirements documents into structured, enterprise-grade specifications. Users upload documents, the system extracts and validates requirements, and generates versioned Functional Specs, Technical Specs, User Stories, and Review Comments through a reusable AI Skill Engine.
objectives: Enable non-technical and technical users to produce structured specs from raw requirements in a single guided workflow.
Surface gaps and open questions automatically without hallucinating missing information.
Generate versioned, editable, and exportable specs via a reusable Skill Engine.
scope: The MVP covers document upload (PDF/DOCX/TXT), requirement extraction, gap detection, generation of four spec types, inline rich-text editing, and Markdown export. Authentication, real-time collaboration, and Jira/Slack integration are out of scope.

features.name: Project Management
features.description: Users can create, view, and soft-delete projects. Each project encapsulates documents, requirements, specs, and gap questions.
acceptance_criteria: User can create a project with a name and optional description.
Dashboard lists all active projects with document count and latest spec type.
Soft delete hides the project from listings without destroying data.

features.name: Document Upload and Parsing
features.description: Users upload PDF, DOCX, or TXT files. The system extracts plain text and stores it per document for downstream processing.
acceptance_criteria: Drag-and-drop and file-picker upload are both supported.
Files larger than 20 MB are rejected with a clear error.
Unsupported formats are rejected with a 400 error.
Extracted text is persisted and linked to the project.

features.name: Requirement Extraction
features.description: After upload, users trigger extraction. The system uses the AI Skill Engine to categorise requirements into functional, non-functional, constraints, assumptions, and stakeholders.
acceptance_criteria: Extracted requirements display confidence badges (High/Medium/Low).
Each requirement shows a source reference tooltip quoting the original document.
Results are persisted and linked to the project.

features.name: Gap Detection
features.description: The system analyses extracted requirements and surfaces open questions grouped by severity (Blocker / Major / Minor). No gaps are hallucinated — if none exist, the array is empty.
acceptance_criteria: Gap questions are displayed in the right panel grouped by severity.
Users can mark each gap as resolved and optionally provide a free-text answer.
Resolved answers are fed into subsequent spec generation.

features.name: Spec Generation
features.description: Users trigger sequential generation of Functional Spec, Technical Spec, User Stories, and Review Comments. Each generation creates a new versioned record.
acceptance_criteria: Each tab populates progressively as its spec arrives.
A version badge shows when a spec has been regenerated (e.g., v2).
All four spec types are persisted with version numbers.

features.name: Inline Editing and Auto-Save
features.description: Generated spec content is editable via a rich-text editor. Changes are auto-saved with a 500 ms debounce.
acceptance_criteria: Editor supports bold, italic, headings, bullet lists, and inline code.
A Saved / Saving… indicator reflects auto-save state.
Edited content persists on page refresh.

features.name: Markdown Export
features.description: Users can export any spec type or all specs as a formatted Markdown file.
acceptance_criteria: Export button downloads a .md file named {project}-{spec-type}-v{n}.md.
Export All produces a single combined Markdown file.
The file is readable without further processing.
